title: willoughbya buddlejifolia
intro: Willoughbya buddlejifolia, also known as the New Zealand olive, is a species of flowering plant in the family Opiliaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, with its leaves, bark, and roots possessing medicinal properties. The plant's extracts have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it beneficial for relieving symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Willoughbya buddlejifolia can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can help to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
